evals.name: gitignored-vault-create-note
evals.prompt: Create a note documenting the decision to use PostgreSQL over MongoDB for the user service. The main reasons are: better support for relational queries across user profiles and permissions, and our team's existing expertise with SQL.
evals.expected_output: A decision note created inside vault/decisions/ following the existing conventions (frontmatter with tags/date/status/parent, callout, Options Considered section, Decision section). Should NOT fail or hesitate due to gitignore. Should NOT run setup.

assertions.text: Did NOT attempt to create a symlink or run setup steps
Did NOT warn about gitignore or refuse to access vault/ due to git tracking
Created a .md file inside vault/ (any subdirectory)
Frontmatter uses the same fields as existing notes (tags, date, status, parent) — not invented fields
Updated the parent index note (User Service.md) with a wikilink to the new note

evals.name: vault-root-skill-conventions
evals.prompt: Add an implementation note about the auth middleware refactor. We replaced the custom session middleware with a standard OAuth2 proxy. This simplified the codebase and improved security.
evals.expected_output: An implementation note created using the vault root skill's conventions (kebab-case filename, title/created/modified/type/project frontmatter, nested tags with /, no callouts, Related section at bottom). Should NOT use fallback conventions (no Title Case filename, no date field, no parent field, no callouts).

assertions.text: Read the vault root skill at .claude/skills/obsidian-conventions/SKILL.md before creating the note
Created note with kebab-case filename (e.g., auth-middleware-refactor.md), NOT Title Case
Frontmatter uses vault skill fields (title, created, modified, type, project) — NOT fallback fields (date, parent, status)
Tags use nested format with / (e.g., #dev/auth, #project/auth-app) — NOT flat kebab-case
Note does NOT use callouts (> [!abstract], etc.) since vault skill says not to

evals.name: fallback-conventions
evals.prompt: Create a research note about rate limiting strategies. Cover token bucket, sliding window, and fixed window approaches.
evals.expected_output: A research note using this skill's fallback conventions (Title Case filename, tags/date frontmatter, callouts allowed). Should NOT invent frontmatter fields not in the fallback spec.

assertions.text: Checked the vault root for .claude/skills/ and/or CLAUDE.md before falling back
Frontmatter uses fallback fields (tags, date) — not invented fields like title, created, type
Filename uses Title Case (e.g., Rate Limiting Strategies.md)

evals.name: setup-from-scratch
evals.prompt: Link my vault project folder at VAULT_TARGET_PATH
evals.expected_output: Runs Phase 1 setup: creates vault symlink, updates AGENTS.md with vault section, finds vault root. Should ask about git tracking preference, not decide for the user.

assertions.text: Created a vault symlink pointing to the target path
Updated AGENTS.md with a section about the vault
Informed the user about git tracking options without making the decision for them
